data.text/markdown:
GLM-HMM Model Recovery

Goal: verify that EM can recover known ground-truth parameters.
  1. Choose a subject & K → load its real trial covariates X
  2. Define true emission weights W and transition matrix A
  3. Simulate synthetic choices on top of the real trial structure
  4. Fit with EM
  5. Compare fitted ↔ true parameters

data.text/html: 1 - Data & model configuration

2 - True parameters (K=2, M=15 features)

15 features loadedEmission weights — drag a value to change it

Sanity check — CategoricalRegressionHMM with true params

Check Value
Emission log-lik (true params) -0.2304
Chance log-lik (−log 3) -1.0986
Gap (> 0 → convention OK) 0.8682
CategoricalRegressionHMM Viterbi acc (best perm) 89.38%
  • If gap > 0 and CategoricalRegressionHMM accuracy is high: emission convention is correct; any issue is inside SoftmaxGLMHMM
  • If gap ≈ 0: convention mismatch between simulation and model
  • If both low: states are simply hard to distinguish from choices alone (need more data or stronger weights)

State inference: filtering - smoothing - Viterbi

True params Fitted params
Viterbi accuracy (all trials) 89.33% 87.79%
